project.name: UIBuilder
project.summary: UIBuilder is a Swift 6 runtime engine for executing UI described by external JSON. The system decodes JSON into ScreenDocument and UINode, renders a SwiftUI tree at runtime, supports state-driven updates, navigation flow, event/action execution, and embeds WKWebView through a SwiftUI wrapper with eval/JavaScript bridge support.
project.core_principle: The project is built as a platform for generating, rendering, modifying, and executing UI as data rather than hand-coding static screens.

contract_layer.purpose: Defines what UI can be generated and how AI should choose supported runtime entities.

parts.UISchema.json: Defines the valid document structure, node types, props, events, actions, expressions, and host.toolbar slots.
parts.UIDecisions.json: Defines semantic guidance for when to use nodes, what to avoid, and how to compose valid screens.

runtime_model.purpose: Defines the in-memory representation of a loaded screen.

parts.ScreenDocument.swift: Root Codable runtime document for one screen.
parts.UINode.swift: Recursive node model representing every UI element in the tree.
parts.UIAction.swift: Action model for runtime side effects.
parts.Expression.swift: Expression language for visibility, enablement, and conditional logic.

runtime_execution.purpose: Executes runtime behavior after a document is decoded.

runtime_rendering.purpose: Transforms the runtime node tree into live SwiftUI views.

parts.UIRenderer.swift: Recursive renderer from UINode to SwiftUI.
parts.NodeStyle.swift: Applies shared prop-driven styling and layout modifiers.

web_runtime.purpose: Embeds and controls live web content inside the runtime system.

parts.WebViewRegistry.swift: Tracks live webviews by id for eval targeting.
parts.Injected toolkit / browser helpers: JavaScript-side tooling layered over embedded webviews.

app_shell.purpose: Hosts the runtime surfaces and internal tools inside the app.

parts.RootView.swift: Top-level app shell and mode/surface switching.
parts.ScreenHostView.swift: Owns the active ScreenDocument and renders the runtime screen.
parts.Bundled screens / resources: Example and entry documents used for runtime loading.

local_ai_sandbox.purpose: Experimental area for on-device local LLM integration inside the app.

parts.LocalLLMView.swift: SwiftUI sandbox surface for local chat and model experiments.
parts.LocalLLMService.swift: MLX-based local model loading and inference service.

system_flows: JSON document -> ScreenDocument/UINode -> validation -> UIRenderer -> live SwiftUI tree
User event -> named handler -> UIActionExecutor -> state/navigation/ui/eval effects
webview node id -> WebViewRegistry -> eval action -> JavaScript execution inside WKWebView
LocalLLMView -> LLMEvaluator -> local inference output -> future use in web/script workflows
reading_order_for_new_sessions: Local AI sandbox if the task involves on-device LLM work

notes.purpose: This file is an architectural orientation map for new sessions.
notes.not_a_real_tree: This file is not intended to mirror the repository filesystem exactly.
notes.not_current_state: Task-specific progress, recent changes, and temporary experiments should be tracked separately.
